goal: wave19-editorial-review: Ship AT1b editorial-review agent + agent discoverability per L12 INTERFACE and Orbita dogfood handoff. Four pillars — no auto-approve, no Stripe/credits, no newsletter archive: (1) Editorial-review agent — extend L12 editorial-supply: POST /api/internal/editorial/review-pending (ADMIN_EMAILS) runs LLM review over pending drafts (status draft/pending from objects store); for each draft write metadata editorial_agent { score 0-100, flags string[], summary, reviewedAt, model? } without changing publish state. Reuse chat/llm.ts (MINIMAX_API_KEY / CHAT_LLM_*); graceful skip when no key (metadata editorial_agent: { skipped: true }). Optional POST /api/internal/editorial/drafts/:id/review for single draft. Tests mock LLM + prove no-key skip. (2) /editorial UI — show agent summary/score/flags on draft cards when metadata present; badge when skipped. Keep View full article + approve/reject unchanged. (3) Agent content index — extend GET /api/v1/content list (and GET /content/:slug where applicable) OR add GET /api/v1/objects/catalog?site= for published public knowledge+community objects with { id, slug, title, objectType, type, human_url, api_url, source: wave12_object }; update GET /api/v1/capabilities agent_action text to document post-publish verify via objects/{id} OR new catalog. Do NOT break legacy knowledge-base entries (source: knowledge_base). Tests prove Orbita verify path lists published Wave 12 objects. (4) Wave 18 follow-up — extend listInteractionsForUser + community listInteractions API to return request_mentor, ask_for_intro, apply kinds so .org detail done-state survives reload (actions already persist). (5) UI P1 polish if time — article footer More in Library/Knowledge related links on .io content-page-layout + .org knowledge-object-view; inline Saved/Followed confirmation on save/follow (subtle, no toast library required). Update docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md, SESSION_HANDOFF.md, L12 INTERFACE.md, .editorial-orbita runbooks (review-pending path). Run pnpm turbo build and pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test. Open ONE draft PR to main. English UI.
summary: Jacky's bot: shipping Wave 19 editorial-review agent + agent discoverability — a POST /api/internal/editorial/review-pending that LLM-scores pending drafts into metadata.editorial_agent (graceful no-key skip, never changes publish state), the /editorial queue UI surfacing agent score/flags/summary, a GET /api/v1/objects/catalog of published Wave 12 objects for Orbita post-publish verify (legacy knowledge_base content index preserved), the Wave 18 follow-up so .org request_mentor/ask_for_intro/apply done-state survives reload, plus optional .io/.org related-links + inline saved/followed polish — one draft PR to main
acceptanceCriteria: (1) Editorial-review agent: POST /api/internal/editorial/review-pending (ADMIN_EMAILS session gate, same requireAdmin pattern as the existing editorial lane) runs an LLM review over pending drafts (status draft/pending, editorial_source set — i.e. the same set returned by listEditorialDrafts) and, for EACH reviewed draft, writes metadata.editorial_agent = { score:0-100, flags:string[], summary:string, reviewedAt:ISO, model?:string } WITHOUT changing status/visibility/publishedSlug (publish state untouched). Reuses apps/backend/src/lanes/chat/llm.ts (resolveLlmConfig/isChatLlmConfigured + the POST {baseUrl}/chat/completions shape) — no second LLM client. GRACEFUL SKIP: when no key is configured (isChatLlmConfigured()===false) OR the LLM errors/returns malformed output, it writes metadata.editorial_agent = { skipped:true } (with an optional reason) and never throws/500s. Optional single-draft POST /api/internal/editorial/drafts/:id/review implemented with the same behavior.

(2) /editorial UI (apps/web-org/components/editorial-queue.tsx): each draft card surfaces metadata.editorial_agent when present — agent summary, score (0-100), and flags[] — in a clearly secondary/agent-attributed block; a 'Skipped' badge shows when editorial_agent.skipped===true. The existing 'View full article' toggle and Approve/Reject buttons are unchanged. A control to trigger review (e.g. a 'Run agent review' button hitting POST /api/internal/editorial/review-pending and refreshing the queue) is acceptable and English-only.

(3) Agent content index for published Wave 12 objects: GET /api/v1/objects/catalog?site=io|org returns published, PUBLIC knowledge + community objects from the object store with entries { id, slug, title, objectType, type, human_url, api_url, source:'wave12_object' } (api_url = /api/v1/objects/{id}; human_url = site/section-appropriate). The legacy GET /api/v1/content list + /content/:slug (knowledge-base markdown) still work unchanged and their entries are tagged source:'knowledge_base' so agents can distinguish the two indexes. GET /api/v1/capabilities agent_action / endpoints text documents the post-publish verify path (read the published object via /api/v1/objects/{id} OR list via the new /api/v1/objects/catalog). Legacy knowledge_base entries are NOT broken (the existing agent-protocol content tests stay green).

(4) Wave 18 follow-up: listInteractionsForUser (apps/backend/src/db/community.ts) and the community listInteractions API (GET /community/interactions, /api + /api/v1) now also return request_mentor, ask_for_intro, and apply interaction kinds (in addition to follow/offer_help/join), so the .org community detail done-state for those verbs survives a reload. The shared communityInteractionKindSchema (packages/shared/src/wave13-community.ts) is extended additively to include those kinds without breaking existing follow/offer_help/join consumers.

(5) OPTIONAL P1 polish (do if low-risk, else explicitly defer in handoff): an article-footer 'More in Library' (/.io content-page-layout) and 'More in Knowledge' (/.org knowledge-object-view) related-links block, and inline, subtle 'Saved'/'Followed' confirmation on save/follow actions (NO toast library added). Light default + dark toggle preserved.

Tests: new backend vitest tests prove (a) review-pending with NO key writes editorial_agent.skipped:true and never 500s; (b) with a MOCKED LLM (fake key + stubbed /chat/completions) it writes editorial_agent.score/flags/summary and does NOT change draft status/visibility; (c) GET /api/v1/objects/catalog lists published Wave 12 objects (the Orbita post-publish verify path) while legacy /content stays intact; (d) listInteractions returns the new kinds. The existing suite stays green.
`pnpm turbo build` passes for ALL build targets (web-io, web-org, backend, combined, shared, content) and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` passes (existing + new tests).

docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md, docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md updated to mark Wave 19 shipped; apps/backend/src/lanes/editorial-supply/INTERFACE.md updated with the review-pending/single-review endpoints + editorial_agent metadata + catalog; .editorial-orbita runbooks (README.md + runbooks/weekly-seed.md) document the post-publish verify path (GET /api/v1/objects/catalog and editorial review-pending).
Exactly ONE draft PR opened to main (not merged, not marked ready). English UI throughout. No Stripe/credits, no newsletter archive, NO auto-approve (review never changes publish state).

tasks.name: backend-wave19
tasks.scopedGoal:
Implement the Wave 19 BACKEND + SHARED slice ONLY (apps/backend/** and packages/shared/**). Do NOT touch apps/web-io/**, apps/web-org/**, apps/combined/**, or docs/** (except apps/backend/src/lanes/editorial-supply/INTERFACE.md which you OWN) — the web UI and top-level docs are separate downstream workers that branch from YOUR branch. Run `pnpm install` first (node_modules usually missing on cloud start).

DELIVERABLE 1 — EDITORIAL-REVIEW AGENT (pillar 1). In apps/backend/src/lanes/editorial-supply/index.ts add:
  - POST /review-pending (so the full path is /api/internal/editorial/review-pending). Gate with the SAME admin check the lane already uses: resolveRequester(c) → requireAdmin(requester) (returns 401 Not authenticated / 403 Forbidden). On success it reviews the pending-draft set. Reuse listEditorialDrafts({ site }) (from ../../db/objects.js) — optionally accept an optional JSON body { site?: 'io'|'org', limit?: number } to scope which drafts; default = all pending/draft editorial drafts. For EACH draft, compute an editorial_agent review and PERSIST it to that object's metadata WITHOUT changing publish state. Persist using updateObjectLifecycle({ id: draft.id, status: draft.status, metadata: { ...draft.metadata, editorial_agent } }) — passing status: draft.status (and NOT passing visibility/publishedSlug) leaves status/visibility/publishedSlug unchanged (see apps/backend/src/db/objects.ts updateObjectLifecycle: nextVisibility defaults to existing, nextPublishedSlug stays when publishedSlug is undefined). editorial_agent shape on success: { score: number 0-100, flags: string[], summary: string, reviewedAt: ISO string, model?: string }. Return JSON { ok:true, reviewed: number, results: Array<{ id, editorial_agent }> }.
  - POST /drafts/:id/review (path /api/internal/editorial/drafts/:id/review): same admin gate; load getObjectById(id); 404 if missing or not an editorial draft (reuse the lane's isEditorialDraft + the draft/pending status guard like GET /drafts/:id); run the same review for that one draft, persist editorial_agent, return { ok:true, draft: <draftDetail with metadata> }.

  REVIEW IMPLEMENTATION: create a NEW module apps/backend/src/lanes/editorial-supply/review.ts exporting an async function reviewDraft(input: { title: string|null, body: string, objectType: string, type: string }): Promise<EditorialAgentReview> that REUSES apps/backend/src/lanes/chat/llm.ts: import { resolveLlmConfig, isChatLlmConfigured, extractAssistantContent }. If !isChatLlmConfigured() return { skipped: true, reviewedAt, reason: 'llm_not_configured' }. Otherwise POST `${config.baseUrl}/chat/completions` with the SAME header/body shape generateChatReply uses (model, messages [system+user], temperature, max_tokens; add reasoning_split:true when provider==='minimax'), instructing the model to return STRICT JSON { score: 0-100 integer (editorial readiness), flags: string[] (e.g. tone, factual-risk, length, off-brand), summary: 1-2 sentence editorial assessment }. Parse defensively with extractAssistantContent + JSON.parse wrapped in try/catch (strip ```json fences). On non-200, fetch throw, malformed/missing fields, or out-of-range score → return { skipped: true, reviewedAt, reason: 'llm_error' } (or 'malformed'). NEVER throw out of reviewDraft and NEVER let the route 500 because of the LLM — the route must succeed even when every draft is skipped. Clamp score to 0-100, coerce flags to string[]. Include model: config.model on success.

  Add the EditorialAgentReview type to packages/shared (e.g. a new packages/shared/src/wave19-editorial.ts exporting an editorialAgentReviewSchema/zod + type covering BOTH the success shape { score, flags, summary, reviewedAt, model? } and the skip shape { skipped:true, reviewedAt, reason? }) and re-export it from the barrel with `export * from './wave19-editorial'` in packages/shared/src/index.ts (follow the existing per-file re-export style; do NOT alter the agent-entry / buildAgentQuickStart / 'Harvest Hub' strings a backend test asserts on).

DELIVERABLE 2 — AGENT CONTENT CATALOG (pillar 3). Add GET /objects/catalog (so /api/v1/objects/catalog) returning published, PUBLIC knowledge + community objects from the Wave 12 object store. Implement it in the OBJECTS lane (apps/backend/src/lanes/objects/index.ts) and REGISTER THE ROUTE BEFORE the existing objectsRouter.get('/objects/:id', ...) at ~L328 so the static 'catalog' segment is not captured as :id (objectsRouter is mounted at BOTH /api and /api/v1). Use the public/anonymous visibility context (the same VisibilityQueryContext shape resolveRequester builds for an anonymous requester — no session user) and call listObjectsForRequester({ request: { site, status:'published', visibility:'public', objectType:<knowledge then community> , limit }, context }) (from ../../db/objects.js) for objectType 'knowledge' and 'community' (concatenate, or omit objectType and filter in code to those two). Map each ObjectRecord to { id, slug: record.publishedSlug, title: record.title ?? record.subject ?? null, objectType, type, human_url, api_url: `${origin}/api/v1/objects/${record.id}`, source: 'wave12_object' }. Resolve site from ?site= (default per host) and origin = site==='org' ? https://ai-transformation.org : https://ai-transformation.io. human_url by section: knowledge on io → `${origin}/library/${slug}`, knowledge on org → `${origin}/knowledge/${slug}`, community → `${origin}/community/${id}` (confirm the real Next.js route segments by inspecting apps/web-io/app and apps/web-org/app; if a section differs, use the actual one and note it in the handoff). Response: { ok:true, site, origin, count, objects: [...] }. Add a zod schema for the catalog entry to packages/shared (extend wave19-editorial.ts or a small wave19 file) and re-export it.
  TAG LEGACY CONTENT: in apps/backend/src/lanes/agent-protocol/content-loader.ts add source:'knowledge_base' to every ContentListEntry (and the ContentDocument) returned by listContent/getContent (extend the ContentListEntry type with `source: 'knowledge_base'`), so the two indexes are distinguishable. Keep existing fields intact — the agent-protocol content tests assert on slug/title/api_url/human_url and must stay green.
  CAPABILITIES TEXT (pillar 3): in apps/backend/src/lanes/agent-protocol/index.ts buildCapabilities(), add an endpoints entry documenting GET /api/v1/objects/catalog (status available, auth none) and update an agent_action/changelog line to tell agents that after a draft is approved/published they can VERIFY it by reading GET /api/v1/objects/{id} or listing GET /api/v1/objects/catalog?site=. Keep API_VERSION/agent-entry text the agent-protocol test asserts on intact unless you also update that test consistently.

DELIVERABLE 3 — WAVE 18 FOLLOW-UP (pillar 4). In apps/backend/src/db/community.ts listInteractionsForUser, change the interaction filter `i.kind IN ('offer_help', 'join')` (~L607) to also include 'request_mentor', 'ask_for_intro', 'apply' so those persisted community_interactions are returned. In packages/shared/src/wave13-community.ts extend communityInteractionKindSchema = z.enum(['follow','offer_help','join']) to ADD 'request_mentor','ask_for_intro','apply' (additive — keep existing values; this widens communityInteractionRecordSchema.kind and the list request kind filter so the new kinds round-trip). Verify the community listInteractions route (communityRouter.get('/community/interactions')) and the shared client (packages/shared/src/index.ts community.listInteractions + its v1 mirror) still typecheck; the kind FILTER param now legitimately accepts the new kinds. Do not regress follow/offer_help/join behavior.

TESTS (apps/backend, vitest — `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test`). Follow the existing integration-test pattern (mkdtemp SQLITE_PATH, vi.resetModules(), dynamic import('../../index.js'), app.request('http://localhost/api/...')). See apps/backend/src/lanes/editorial-supply/editorial-supply.test.ts, chat/chat.test.ts and community/community.test.ts for the no-key/mocked-LLM patterns (they delete MINIMAX_API_KEY/CHAT_LLM_API_KEY/OPENAI_API_KEY to force no-key, and spy on globalThis.fetch for the /chat/completions URL after setting a fake key). Add tests that: (a) WITHOUT a key, POST /api/internal/editorial/review-pending (as an admin session — reuse the lane test's admin-session helper) writes editorial_agent.skipped:true to each pending draft and returns 200 (never 500), and the draft status/visibility are UNCHANGED; (b) WITH a fake key + mocked /chat/completions returning valid JSON {score,flags,summary}, review-pending writes editorial_agent with score/flags/summary and the draft remains status:'draft' (publish state untouched); (c) a malformed/erroring mocked LLM still results in editorial_agent.skipped (no 500); (d) GET /api/v1/objects/catalog returns published public Wave 12 objects (seed one published knowledge object) with source:'wave12_object' and api_url /api/v1/objects/{id}, while GET /api/v1/content still returns the legacy list tagged source:'knowledge_base'; (e) after persisting a request_mentor/ask_for_intro/apply action (via the existing /community/actions route) GET /community/interactions returns those kinds. Keep the existing suite green.

Run `pnpm install`, then `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend build` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/shared build` (if it builds) must pass. Commit to your branch and push. Do NOT open a PR. Your handoff MUST list: exact new endpoint paths + request/response shapes, new files (review.ts, wave19-editorial.ts), the editorial_agent metadata shape, the catalog entry shape + how human_url/api_url are built (and the real route segments you confirmed), the communityInteractionKindSchema additions, the capabilities/changelog text changes, and the build+test results incl. the new tests. The web + docs workers branch from YOUR branch, so be explicit — your shapes are their contract.
tasks.brief:
READ FIRST: apps/backend/src/lanes/editorial-supply/INTERFACE.md (the L12 contract — you OWN and will update it), apps/backend/src/lanes/editorial-supply/index.ts (existing draft create/list/get/approve/reject + requireAdmin + isEditorialDraft + draftListItem/draftDetail), apps/backend/src/lanes/chat/llm.ts (resolveLlmConfig/isChatLlmConfigured/extractAssistantContent + the /chat/completions call shape — copy its no-throw discipline), apps/backend/src/db/objects.ts (listEditorialDrafts, getObjectById, updateObjectLifecycle [pass status: existing.status to keep publish state], listObjectsForRequester, VisibilityQueryContext, ObjectRecord), apps/backend/src/lanes/objects/index.ts (resolveRequester, objectsRouter routes — add /objects/catalog BEFORE /objects/:id), apps/backend/src/lanes/agent-protocol/index.ts + content-loader.ts (buildCapabilities, listContent/getContent, ContentListEntry — add source:'knowledge_base'), apps/backend/src/db/community.ts listInteractionsForUser (~L607 IN clause) + upsertCommunityAction + getExtendedInteractionById, packages/shared/src/wave13-community.ts (communityInteractionKindSchema ~L55), packages/shared/src/index.ts barrel (per-file `export * from './waveNN-...'`; wave17-newsletter.ts is intentionally NOT re-exported — but you DO re-export your new wave19 file). Load .agents/skills/lane-backend-core/SKILL.md + .agents/skills/lane-shared/SKILL.md + .agents/skills/lane-editorial-supply/SKILL.md.

MOUNTING: app.route in apps/backend/src/index.ts mounts editorialRouter at /api/internal/editorial; objectsRouter + agentProtocolRouter at /api/v1 (agentProtocolRouter ALSO only at /api/v1). objectsRouter is at both /api and /api/v1. Keep backend host-agnostic; backend + combined run via tsx (packages/shared exports .ts); backend tsc excludes *.test.ts. English code. NO auto-approve: review NEVER changes publish state. Do NOT touch apps/web-* / apps/combined / docs/** (except editorial-supply/INTERFACE.md). The downstream web + docs workers branch from YOUR pushed branch, so your endpoint shapes + editorial_agent metadata + catalog entry shape are their contract — make the handoff explicit about exact names and signatures.
acceptance: POST /api/internal/editorial/review-pending (ADMIN_EMAILS gate) reviews pending/draft editorial drafts and writes metadata.editorial_agent { score 0-100, flags[], summary, reviewedAt, model? } via updateObjectLifecycle WITHOUT changing status/visibility/publishedSlug; graceful skip → { skipped:true } when no key/LLM error; never 500. Optional POST /api/internal/editorial/drafts/:id/review behaves the same for one draft. Review logic in apps/backend/src/lanes/editorial-supply/review.ts reusing chat/llm.ts (no second client).

GET /api/v1/objects/catalog?site= returns published PUBLIC knowledge+community Wave 12 objects { id, slug, title, objectType, type, human_url, api_url:/api/v1/objects/{id}, source:'wave12_object' }; route registered before /objects/:id. Legacy /api/v1/content + /content/:slug unchanged and tagged source:'knowledge_base'. /api/v1/capabilities documents the post-publish verify path (objects/{id} or catalog).

listInteractionsForUser + GET /community/interactions return request_mentor, ask_for_intro, apply in addition to follow/offer_help/join; communityInteractionKindSchema extended additively in packages/shared/src/wave13-community.ts.

New backend tests prove: no-key review skip (editorial_agent.skipped, status unchanged, no 500), mocked-LLM review writes score/flags/summary without changing publish state, malformed LLM falls back to skip, catalog lists published Wave 12 objects while legacy content stays intact, listInteractions returns the new kinds. `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend build`+`test` pass with no regressions; `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/shared` typechecks/builds.

New shared types re-exported from the barrel WITHOUT altering the agent-entry/'Harvest Hub' text the agent-protocol test asserts on. No files under apps/web-*, apps/combined, or docs/** (besides editorial-supply/INTERFACE.md) changed.
tasks.verify:

## Automated

- Assert: review-pending with NO key → every draft gets editorial_agent.skipped:true, 200 not 500, draft status unchanged; with fake key + mocked /chat/completions valid JSON → editorial_agent {score,flags,summary} written, status still 'draft'; malformed mocked LLM → skipped, no 500. GET /api/v1/objects/catalog lists a seeded published object with source:'wave12_object'; /api/v1/content still returns knowledge_base entries. listInteractions returns request_mentor/ask_for_intro/apply after a /community/actions POST.
## Manual / code inspection

## Gotchas

tasks.name: web-org-wave19
tasks.scopedGoal:
Implement the Wave 19 .org WEB slice in apps/web-org/** ONLY. Your starting branch already contains the backend + shared changes (the upstream backend-wave19 handoff is pasted into this prompt — read it for the EXACT editorial_agent metadata shape, the catalog entry shape, and the new interaction kinds). Do NOT modify apps/backend/**, packages/**, apps/web-io/**, apps/combined/**, or docs/** — adapt the UI to what the shared types/endpoints provide and note any gap in your handoff. Run `pnpm install` first (node_modules usually missing on cloud start).

DELIVERABLE 1 — /editorial AGENT REVIEW UI (pillar 2, the core of this task). In apps/web-org/components/editorial-queue.tsx, surface the agent review on each draft card. The draft objects already carry `metadata` (EditorialDraft.metadata: Record<string,unknown>|null), and after Wave 19 backend, metadata.editorial_agent holds either { score:number, flags:string[], summary:string, reviewedAt:string, model?:string } OR { skipped:true, reviewedAt:string, reason?:string }. In DraftCard, render a SECONDARY, clearly agent-attributed block (e.g. 'Agent review' label) when metadata.editorial_agent is present: show the summary, the score (e.g. 'Score 78/100'), and the flags as small chips; when editorial_agent.skipped===true show a muted 'Agent review skipped' badge (optionally the reason). Keep the existing 'View full article' toggle and Approve/Reject buttons EXACTLY as they are. Add a 'Run agent review' control: a button in the queue header (EditorialQueue) that POSTs to `${apiBase()}/api/internal/editorial/review-pending` (credentials:'include', method:'POST'), shows pending/error state, and on success re-runs loadQueue() so the new editorial_agent metadata appears on the cards. Use the existing resolveClientApiUrl/apiBase + fetch + ACCESS_DENIED conventions in the file; English-only, editorial visual language (muted, secondary; not a hero element). Optionally type editorial_agent via the shared editorialAgentReviewSchema type if it imports cleanly, else a local type is fine.

DELIVERABLE 2 — OPTIONAL P1 POLISH on .org (pillar 5; do if low-risk, else defer + note in handoff): (a) In apps/web-org/components/knowledge-object-view.tsx add an article-footer 'More in Knowledge' related-links block (a small list of other knowledge objects/links — you may derive related items from existing data already loaded on the page or a lightweight existing list source; keep it simple, no new backend dependency). (b) Add inline, subtle 'Followed'/'Saved' confirmation text/checkmark on the follow/save affordances (e.g. in the community/knowledge interaction buttons) — a brief inline state change, NOT a toast library. Preserve light default + dark toggle. If either materially increases risk/scope, SKIP it and say so in your handoff (pillar 5 is explicitly optional).

CONSTRAINTS: English-only UI; preserve light default + dark toggle; editorial visual language (load .agents/skills/editorial-ui + ux-copy before writing copy). Editorial queue is admin-gated (ACCESS_DENIED 401/403 handling already exists — keep it). Run `pnpm install` first, then ensure `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build` AND typecheck pass. Commit + push your branch. Do NOT open a PR. Your handoff MUST list: how the editorial_agent block + skipped badge + 'Run agent review' button render, whether the optional .org polish was done or deferred, and the build/typecheck results.

tasks.name: web-io-wave19
tasks.scopedGoal:
Implement the Wave 19 .io WEB slice in apps/web-io/** ONLY (pillar 5 — OPTIONAL P1 polish). Your starting branch already contains the backend + shared changes (the upstream backend-wave19 handoff is pasted into this prompt). Do NOT modify apps/backend/**, packages/**, apps/web-org/**, apps/combined/**, or docs/**. Run `pnpm install` first (node_modules usually missing on cloud start).

DELIVERABLES (pillar 5 polish; keep it small, cohesive, low-risk):
  (1) ARTICLE-FOOTER 'MORE IN LIBRARY' RELATED LINKS. In apps/web-io/components/content-page-layout.tsx (the shared .io library/article detail layout, route apps/web-io/app/library/[slug]/page.tsx) add a footer 'More in Library' block listing a few other Library entries/links. Derive the related items from data ALREADY available to the page or an existing lightweight content list source in apps/web-io/lib (do NOT add a new backend call or new dependency); exclude the current article; cap at ~3-5 links; link to /library/<slug>. Keep it visually secondary (editorial footer style), English-only.
  (2) INLINE SAVED/FOLLOWED CONFIRMATION. On the .io save/follow affordances (find the save/bookmark + follow buttons used on library cards/detail and the personal cockpit — likely in apps/web-io/components and apps/web-io/lib), add a SUBTLE inline confirmation when the action succeeds (e.g. the button label/icon switches to 'Saved'/'Followed' with a brief inline check) — NO toast library, no new dependency. Use the existing client/hook that performs the save/follow; just reflect success inline. Preserve light default + dark toggle and existing behavior (toggling off still works).

If either deliverable would materially increase risk/scope (e.g. no clean related-items source, or save/follow state is deeply entangled), implement the safe one and explicitly DEFER the other in your handoff — pillar 5 is optional and must not break the build.

CONSTRAINTS: English-only UI; editorial visual language (load .agents/skills/editorial-ui + ux-copy); preserve light default + dark toggle. Run `pnpm install` first, then ensure `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io build` AND typecheck pass. Commit + push your branch. Do NOT open a PR. Your handoff MUST list which deliverables were done vs deferred, the components touched, where related links + inline confirmation were added, and the build/typecheck results.

tasks.name: integrate-wave19
tasks.scopedGoal:
Produce the single integrated Wave 19 deliverable branch and open ONE draft PR to main. You start from orch/wave19-editorial-review/web-org-wave19 (which already contains the backend-wave19 commits as an ancestor + the .org web work). You must MERGE IN the .io web work from orch/wave19-editorial-review/web-io-wave19 (which shares the SAME backend-wave19 ancestor and touches only apps/web-io/** — disjoint from web-org, so conflicts are unlikely). Run `pnpm install` first.

STEPS:
1. `git merge orch/wave19-editorial-review/web-io-wave19` into the current branch, preserving both parents. The two web branches edit disjoint app dirs (apps/web-io vs apps/web-org) over the same backend/shared ancestor, so expect a clean merge; if a conflict arises it will be trivial (keep BOTH sides' app-specific changes). Do NOT drop the backend/shared changes.
2. `pnpm install`, then run `pnpm turbo build` — it MUST pass for ALL targets (web-io, web-org, backend, combined, shared, content) — and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` — it MUST pass (existing suite + the new Wave 19 review/catalog/interactions tests). Fix any integration-only breakage (e.g. import drift) but do NOT change feature behavior.
3. UPDATE DOCS (read the three upstream handoffs — backend + both web — for exact endpoint/metadata/route names): 
   - docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md: mark Wave 19 shipped, summarizing the pillars — (1) editorial-review agent POST /api/internal/editorial/review-pending (+ optional /drafts/:id/review) writing metadata.editorial_agent {score,flags,summary,reviewedAt,model?} with graceful no-key skip and NEVER changing publish state (no auto-approve); (2) /editorial queue UI surfacing agent score/flags/summary + skipped badge + Run-agent-review button; (3) GET /api/v1/objects/catalog of published Wave 12 objects (source:'wave12_object') for Orbita post-publish verify, legacy /content tagged source:'knowledge_base', capabilities documents the verify path; (4) Wave 18 follow-up — listInteractions now returns request_mentor/ask_for_intro/apply so .org done-state survives reload; (5) optional .io/.org P1 polish (related links + inline saved/followed) — note done vs deferred per the web handoffs. Note Wave 20+ direction (newsletter pilot / agent credits ≥50 users) is next.
   - apps/backend/src/lanes/editorial-supply/INTERFACE.md: if backend-wave19 didn't already, add the review-pending + single-review endpoints, the editorial_agent metadata contract, and a pointer to GET /api/v1/objects/catalog. (backend-wave19 owns this file; only fill gaps.)
   - .editorial-orbita/README.md + .editorial-orbita/runbooks/weekly-seed.md: add a 'post-publish verify' step documenting GET /api/v1/objects/catalog?site= (and reading /api/v1/objects/{id}) so the Orbita client can confirm a published object, and mention the founder can run POST /api/internal/editorial/review-pending to get agent scores before approving.
   - Create docs/waves/wave19-editorial-review.md capturing scope + Definition of Done for the four+one pillars (mirror the style of docs/waves/wave18-platform-depth.md).
4. Commit, push EXACTLY your branch, and open a DRAFT PR to main titled 'Wave 19: editorial-review agent + agent discoverability (review-pending + objects catalog + interaction kinds)'. The PR body must summarize each pillar, list `pnpm turbo build` (all targets) + `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` results, and explicitly note: NO auto-approve (review never changes publish state), graceful no-key skip, and which pillar-5 polish items were done vs deferred, and that live LLM/admin-session/browser states were type/build/unit-test-verified (no browser MCP or LLM key in cloud env). Do NOT merge, do NOT mark ready. Check for .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md and use it if present.

English docs/code. Do NOT edit packages/shared/src/index.ts agent-entry/'Harvest Hub' text (a backend agent-protocol test asserts on it).
tasks.brief: This is an integration + docs + PR task (the planner does not code, so this worker finalizes). Topology is a DIAMOND: backend-wave19 → {web-io-wave19, web-org-wave19} → you. Your starting branch is web-org-wave19 (backend ancestor + .org work); you merge in web-io-wave19 (.io work, same backend ancestor, disjoint apps dir). Read the THREE upstream handoffs (backend + both web; pasted into your prompt) for exact endpoint/metadata/route names + which optional polish shipped, and reflect them accurately in docs + PR body. zbpack on Zeabur must list every deploy package, but you are NOT changing deploy config — just verify the monorepo builds for all targets. node_modules are usually missing on cloud start — run `pnpm install` first. Keep merge history with both parents.

docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md mark Wave 19 shipped (pillars summarized, no-auto-approve noted); editorial-supply/INTERFACE.md documents review-pending + editorial_agent + catalog; .editorial-orbita README + weekly-seed runbook document the post-publish verify path; docs/waves/wave19-editorial-review.md created
Exactly ONE DRAFT PR opened to main (not merged, not marked ready) summarizing all pillars + build/test results + which pillar-5 items shipped vs deferred

tasks.name: verify-wave19
tasks.scopedGoal: Verify the integrated Wave 19 branch against the planner acceptanceCriteria. Run `pnpm install`, `pnpm turbo build` (confirm ALL build targets pass — record them), and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` (confirm pass, no regressions, incl. the new Wave 19 tests). Then confirm with execution + code-inspection evidence that each pillar is implemented: (1) POST /api/internal/editorial/review-pending (ADMIN_EMAILS gate) writes metadata.editorial_agent {score,flags,summary,reviewedAt,model?} to pending drafts WITHOUT changing status/visibility/publishedSlug (verify via the new tests + code inspection of updateObjectLifecycle usage), graceful no-key skip → {skipped:true} with no 500, review logic in editorial-supply/review.ts reusing chat/llm.ts (no second LLM client); optional /drafts/:id/review present. (2) apps/web-org/components/editorial-queue.tsx renders the editorial_agent summary/score/flags + skipped badge + a Run-agent-review button, with View-full-article + Approve/Reject unchanged. (3) GET /api/v1/objects/catalog lists published PUBLIC Wave 12 objects { id, slug, title, objectType, type, human_url, api_url:/api/v1/objects/{id}, source:'wave12_object' } (registered before /objects/:id), legacy /api/v1/content + /content/:slug still work and are tagged source:'knowledge_base', and /api/v1/capabilities documents the post-publish verify path — confirm via the new catalog test (the Orbita verify path) and the still-green agent-protocol content tests. (4) listInteractionsForUser + GET /community/interactions return request_mentor/ask_for_intro/apply (additive communityInteractionKindSchema) — confirm via the interactions test. (5) optional .io content-page-layout 'More in Library' + .org knowledge-object-view 'More in Knowledge' related links + inline Saved/Followed confirmation: report done vs deferred honestly. (docs) docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md mark Wave 19 shipped; editorial-supply/INTERFACE.md + .editorial-orbita runbooks document review-pending + the catalog verify path; docs/waves/wave19-editorial-review.md exists. Confirm exactly ONE draft PR to main (not merged/ready), English UI, and the invariants: NO auto-approve (review never changes publish state), no Stripe/credits, no newsletter archive. The cloud env has no browser MCP and no LLM key/admin session, so live UI rendering and live LLM scoring cannot be pixel-verified — verify those via build + code/route inspection + the backend tests and mark unit-test-verified or type-check-only honestly. Report execution evidence per criterion; do not pattern-match. Flag any missing or partial criterion as a finding.
acceptance: Verification section includes execution evidence (pnpm turbo build output for all targets, backend test output incl. the new Wave 19 review/catalog/interactions tests, and per-pillar code/route inspection) for every planner acceptance criterion, plus an honest done/deferred report on the optional pillar-5 polish and confirmation of the no-auto-approve invariant and the single draft PR to main
